summary: Iter 85 REVIEW verification — all 6 review items PASS end-to-end. Backend: 8/9 tests passed, 1 skipped (no hourly-mode Kankani employees to sample). (1) Designation Master seed — GET /api/admin/masters?type=designation&company_id=cmp_cb39e488a0 returns 17 unique designations (BEAM GATTER, BEEM GATTER, CLEANER, DISPATCH, FITTER, GREY CHECKER, GREY HELPER, HELER, HELPER, SUPERVISOR, TAKLER, WATCH MAN, WEAVER, WINDER, YARN HELPER, YARN PREPARTARY INCHARGE, YARN SUPERVISOR). (2) /employee-add — Designation label + auto-uppercase TextInput + 17 tappable chips rendered with testID='des-opt-<NAME>'; tapping a chip calls onChange(name). Verified via screenshot. (3) salary_mode formulas — unit-tested _actual_salary_row_compute directly: daily mode basic_salary=basic×p_days (500×20=10000, no ÷month_days), w_basic_salary=basic×p_hours/duty_hrs (500×40/8=2500); hourly mode basic_salary=basic×p_hours (100×40=4000); monthly regression 30000×(20/30)=20000. Also verified against a LIVE Kankani 2025-11 run — daily-mode rows correctly use `basic × p_days` formula (5 rows sampled, all match to ±0.05). (4) Biometric auto-transfer — Live Kankani 2025-11 actual-salary-process run rows: p_days == grid.totals.total_days_int (capped by DOJ/exit_date max_p_days) and p_hours == grid.totals.total_extra_hrs for all 29 employees where grid entries exist. Cross-referenced /api/admin/attendance/monthly-grid/{cid}/{month} response payload (employees[].totals.total_days_int / total_extra_hrs). (5) Compliance column hide — PUT compliance-policy with enabled_allowances=['basic','hra'] then POST compliance-salary-runs; API row payload has enabled_allowances=['basic','hra'] and conveyance/medical/special/others=0.0 for all 29 rows. Frontend screenshot of compliance-salary-run detail confirms header row is `Code | Name | Present Days | M.Basic | M.HRA | M.Gross | Basic | HRA | Gross | Wage Base | PF (E) | PF (Er) | ESI (E) | ESI (Er) | PT | ...` — M.Conv/M.Med/M.Spl/M.Others/Conv/Med/Spl/Others* correctly hidden, column alignment preserved. (6) Regression — PATCH /admin/actual-salary-process/{run_id}/row with p_days=15 on a biometric run returns 200 with row.p_days=15 (clamped to max_p_days when applicable).
action_items: No blocking action items — all 6 Iter-85 review items pass functional verification.

OPTIONAL: TestActualSalaryProcessLive.test_salary_mode_hourly_rows_use_hourly_formula skipped because Kankani has no hourly-mode employees in 2025-11. If hourly-mode payroll is a supported production case, seed at least one hourly employee to cover this path in future regressions.

OPTIONAL: /api/admin/attendance/monthly-grid/{cid}/{month} response for Kankani 2025-11 has all totals=0 (no biometric punch data seeded for Nov 2025). The p_days=total_days_int equality passes trivially. To validate the transfer with real values, seed some November punches or test against a month with actual biometric data.

OPTIONAL: server.py._actual_salary_row_compute signature reads salary_mode straight from row — confirm that the actual-salary-process POST correctly persists emp.get('salary_mode') onto the row (verified at server.py:17001, row['salary_mode'] = emp.get('salary_mode') or 'monthly' — sound).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:16785-16818 — _actual_salary_row_compute cleanly branches on salary_mode ∈ {daily,hourly,monthly}. Daily & hourly correctly bypass month_days divisor as spec'd. Rounding to 2dp is consistent.

server.py:16963-16964 — Biometric auto-transfer uses `total_days_int` and `total_extra_hrs` with correct fallback (`is not None` check) so 0 doesn't fall back to the legacy `present_days`/`hours` fields. Correct.
server.py:17061-17108 — patch_actual_salary_row now unconditionally accepts p_days/p_hours (src_lock removed) and applies max_p_days cap. Docstring updated to reflect Iter-85 unlock.
employee-add.tsx:824-887 — DesignationPicker fetches /admin/masters?type=designation, dedupes/uppercases names, sorts alphabetically; chip has testID='des-opt-<NAME>'. Sound implementation.

compliance-salary-run.tsx:896-931 — Column-hide via `has(k)` mask applies to BOTH header and data cells; `basic` is always kept even if not in enabled_allowances (statutory floor), matching backend behavior at utils/compliance_salary.py.

compliance-salary-run.tsx has grown to 1764 lines. The Compliance Grid + updatePresentDays helper (~200 lines) remains a natural refactor candidate into a sub-component (called out in iteration_85_deeptest as well).
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iter85_review.py (new — 9 tests across 4 classes covering all 6 review items)

success_rate.backend: 100% (8/8 executed; 1 skipped — no hourly-mode Kankani employees)
success_rate.frontend: 100% — items #2 and #5 verified via live browser: 17 chips rendered on /employee-add, column-hide observed on /compliance-salary-run detail header

seed_data_creation: Created transient records only: 2 actual-salary-process runs and 3 compliance-salary-runs for Kankani 2025-11 to exercise the compute paths. Compliance policy was temporarily set to enabled_allowances=['basic','hra'] and restored to full set ['basic','hra','conveyance','medical','special','others'] at test teardown. No orphaned users/firms created.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 85 review items 1–6 all verified. Test suite lives at /app/backend/tests/test_iter85_review.py. To rerun: `pytest backend/tests/test_iter85_review.py -v`. Kankani firm has NO biometric punch data for 2025-11 in the current DB (grid.totals.total_days_int=0 for all 29 employees), so the biometric auto-transfer test only asserts the pass-through semantics; if a future test needs to see non-zero p_days on a fresh run, seed punches first or use a month with real punch data. Compliance column-hide is toggled per-firm via PUT /admin/companies/{cid}/compliance-policy — the enabled_allowances field is copied onto every row of subsequent compliance-salary-runs, and the frontend header uses row[0].enabled_allowances to build the has(k) column mask.
